data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Streamlined user experience with reduced cognitive load by limiting choices on any given page.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Elements are placed and sized to optimize for ease of clicking and interaction.
data.technical_spec: Larger clickable areas for primary actions, frequently used actions positioned near the user's point of focus.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Visually attractive interfaces are designed to make systems more usable and plausible.
data.technical_spec: High-quality visuals, consistent color schemes, clean typography.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: The web experience accounts for the average memory capacity by chunking information.
data.technical_spec: Card layouts, segmented flows in onboarding processes.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Design patterns are familiar to users based on their experience with other Microsoft products.
data.technical_spec: Consistency in navigation, UI components across platforms.
